title: Ormerodia sagittata
taxonomy: Ormerodia sagittata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. This family consists of sedge plants, which are commonly found in wetlands and aquatic environments. The plant's classification further places it in the genus Ormerodia, indicating its unique characteristics and characteristics that distinguish it from other plants in the same family. The Cyperaceae family is part of the larger monocot clade, which includes other plants with one cotyledon in their seeds. Ormerodia sagittata is a relatively rare and lesser-known species, which may contribute to its classification as a distinct entity within the Cyperaceae family.
